failingTest: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns undefined instead of the prosody definition; getTagDef('Break') returns undefined instead of the break definition
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — change getTagDef comparison to case-insensitive
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase and mixed-case test cases
fixApproach: In getTagDef(), normalize the comparison by lowercasing both the input tagName and the definition's tag property: `t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase()`. This single-line change makes all lookups case-insensitive. isTagSupportedByProvider() benefits automatically since it delegates to getTagDef(). Add test cases for uppercase ('PROSODY'), mixed-case ('Break'), and already-lowercase ('emphasis') lookups, plus a guard that nonexistent tags still return undefined.
steps: 1. In apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts, change the getTagDef comparison from `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase()`

2. In apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts, add test cases: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns prosody def, getTagDef('Break') returns break def, getTagDef('emphasis') returns emphasis def, getTagDef('nonexistent') returns undefined, and isTagSupportedByProvider('PROSODY', provider) matches isTagSupportedByProvider('prosody', provider)
3. Run `npm run test` to verify all existing and new tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive so SSML tag lookups work regardless of casing (PROSODY, Break, emphasis all resolve correctly).
